description: All HVAC terminal equipment is set, final connections are made, systems are balanced and tested, and Baldwin County mechanical final inspection is passed before certificate of occupancy is pursued.

items.label: Drywall and paint are complete in all areas where HVAC registers, grilles, and thermostats will be set — no trim-out before finish surfaces are done
All rough-in penetrations confirmed still open and unobstructed (not accidentally drywalled over or patched shut)
Equipment pads, curbs, and attic platforms for air handlers and equipment verified plumb, level, and structurally adequate
Condensate drain lines from rough-in confirmed pitched correctly (min 1/8 in per foot per IRC M2202) and daylight termination accessible
HVAC sub has approved equipment schedule on-site matching approved design — no field substitutions without superintendent sign-off
All duct boots confirm correct size and orientation match supply/return schedule before grilles are ordered
Air handler(s) set on platform or in closet, level within 1/4 inch, vibration isolation pads installed, service clearances per manufacturer (min 30 in front of filter access)
Condenser(s) set on pad, level within 1/4 inch, minimum 12 in clearance on all sides (24 in on service side), secured against wind uplift per Baldwin County coastal wind zone requirements
Refrigerant line sets connected, insulated full length with closed-cell foam minimum 1/2-in wall, no kinks or unsupported spans greater than 4 ft in unconditioned space
System charged to manufacturer specification; refrigerant type and charge weight recorded on equipment data sheet in job file
Electrical disconnect at condenser confirmed correct amperage, weatherproof cover, within sight of unit per NEC 440.14
Condensate primary and secondary drain pans inspected — both connected, secondary pan overflow switch wired to shut down unit on high water
Gas furnace connections (where applicable) pressure-tested at rough-in confirmed; flex connector at unit within 6 in of appliance connection, shutoff valve accessible
All supply registers and return grilles installed with correct CFM-rated size per schedule, flush to finish surface, no visible gaps between grille frame and drywall or ceiling
Register and grille finish (brushed nickel, oil-rubbed bronze, painted, etc.) matches hardware selections approved by client — verified by superintendent before install
All thermostats and smart controls (Ecobee, Nest, or specified brand) mounted level, wired per manufacturer, programmed with correct zone assignments, and labeled
Zoning dampers (where specified) confirmed operational — each zone opens and closes fully on call from corresponding thermostat
ERV or HRV unit (where specified) core installed, intake and exhaust connected to exterior with weatherproof caps, controls wired and functional
Attic access panels and equipment access doors confirmed installed, insulated, and airtight per ACCA Manual D requirements
Static pressure measured at each system and compared against design target — external static pressure within 10% of ACCA Manual D design value
All supply diffusers balanced with flow hood or anemometer readings documented by zone; no single room deviating more than 15% from design CFM
Return air path confirmed adequate for each zone — no room dependent solely on door undercut for return unless undercut is minimum 1-in clearance and verified adequate
System cycling test run minimum 30 minutes per zone in both heating and cooling mode; no abnormal noise, vibration, or fault codes logged
Delta-T across evaporator coil measured and within manufacturer specification (typically 18–22°F) confirming correct charge and airflow
Drain pan pour test performed — water placed in primary pan, confirmed draining; float switch trips unit within 60 seconds of secondary pan filling
All access panels, filter doors, and electrical covers reinstalled and secured after commissioning
Baldwin County mechanical final inspection scheduled and passed — inspection card signed by inspector and scanned to job Drive folder
Equipment data plates photographed and uploaded to job file (model, serial, refrigerant type, charge, electrical data for each unit)
ENERGY STAR or AHRI certificate for each system on file confirming SEER2/HSPF2 ratings meet or exceed permit specification
Manual J load calculation on file matches installed equipment capacity — no system more than 15% oversized per ACCA guidelines
All refrigerant-containing equipment documented per EPA Section 608 requirements; technician certification number recorded in job file
Attic insulation at air handler platform and all flex duct confirmed undisturbed and intact after trim-out work — no compressed or displaced batt sections
All register and grille screws tight, no rattling when system is running at high speed
No visible flex duct sags greater than 1/2 in per foot of run confirmed from attic walk-through after trim-out
Condensate line termination point clean, no staining on soffit or exterior — splash block or piped extension in place where termination is at foundation
All penetrations through fire-rated assemblies (garage-to-living, floor-to-floor) re-sealed with listed fire caulk after trim-out activity
Exterior refrigerant line set cover (Linehide or painted conduit, per elevation spec) installed flush, painted to match trim color, no exposed insulation visible
Client-facing equipment areas (laundry closets, utility rooms) left broom-clean, no copper scraps, wire ties, or packaging left in attic or mechanical spaces
Superintendent final walk confirms zero punch items before scheduling client walkthrough
Client walkthrough conducted with HVAC sub or superintendent — thermostat operation, filter locations, and zone map explained in person
Filter size and replacement schedule documented on laminated card installed inside air handler door or utility closet
Warranty documentation for each piece of equipment (unit, thermostat, controls) collected, organized, and handed to client in homeowner binder
Manufacturer startup warranty registration completed by HVAC sub on client's behalf — confirmation numbers recorded in job file
Client signs HVAC trim-out completion acknowledgment in Palmetto's project management system before phase is closed
